methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $127M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $1.0B - $1.8B.

private.name: Bedrock Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.75B

private.name: Dexterity
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.65B

private.name: The Bot Company
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B)

data.narrative:
Fulfil is a Series B robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $127M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparison universe of nine private peers (including Bedrock Robotics, Dexterity, Hadrian, and Nimble Robotics) and one public comparable, providing directional context for frontier-tech robotics valuations.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using total capital raised as a proxy base, applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, implying a valuation of $241M. Peer-median analysis across four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies yields a median valuation of $1.6B, with a range spanning $1.0B (Nimble Robotics) to $1.8B (Bedrock Robotics).

The two methods produce a wide spread reflecting stage and maturity variance within the peer set. The stage-progression method anchors to capital efficiency; the peer-median reflects recent market pricing for comparable-stage robotics companies. Triangulating both approaches yields a median valuation of $933M with a range of $205M to $1.9B. Key uncertainties include unit economics and customer concentration relative to the peer set, both material to justifying positioning within this range.
